pr.title: Match ithammerhead & link, match some itscopebeam functions
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Implemented and linked/matched Hammer Head item code, plus matched selected Super Scope beam functions and data typing. `ithammerhead.c` was promoted from `NonMatching` to `Matching`; the decomp.dev report credited 9 new matches, including `it_80298ED0`, `it_80299C48`, Hammer Head anim/throw helpers, Hammer Head `.data`/`.sdata2`, and Super Scope beam `.sdata`. Evidence is from the diff and bot report; there was no PR body or human review feedback in this slice.

key_files.reason: Added matched Hammer Head state table, spawn/setup routine, thrown logic, and lifetime anim callbacks.
Added typed Super Scope beam attribute structs and implemented `it_80298ED0`; switched item var usage from `starrodstar` to `scopebeam`.
Renamed Hammer Head attribute field to `initial_velocity` and added `itScopeBeam_ItemVars`.
Added `scopebeam` member to the `Item` item-var union.
Renamed thrown callback symbol and converted matched local `.sdata2` constants to local `@` labels.
Small matching-oriented refactor of `itFoods_Logic18_Spawned` with `getRandMax`, `HSD_GObj*`, and stack padding changes.

what_changed: `ithammerhead.c` replaced placeholders with real code for `it_80299C48`, `it_80299D7C`, `itHammerHead_Logic40_Thrown`, `itHammerhead_UnkMotion2_Anim`, and `itHammerhead_UnkMotion3_Anim`.
Added `ItemStateTable it_803F6640[]` for Hammer Head with Anim/Phys/Coll function pointers for four states.
Hammer Head spawn code now builds a `SpawnItem`, calls `Item_80268B18`, applies `attrs->initial_velocity` to velocity, sets facing direction, and links debug ownership through `db_80225DD8`.
Renamed `it_3F14_Logic40_Thrown` to `itHammerHead_Logic40_Thrown` in symbols, headers, item logic table references, and collision callback use sites.

`itsscopebeam.c` added local `BeamFloats` and `ScopeBeamAttrs` structs, implemented `it_80298ED0`, sets owner, chooses item state through an explicit switch, sets velocity/scale/lifetime, and uses `HSD_JObjSetScale`.
Super Scope beam item vars now use `ip->xDD4_itemVar.scopebeam.x0` instead of reusing `starrodstar.x0`; `itSscopebeam_UnkMotion9_Anim` now reads typed `data->lifetime` and `data->scale`.
`itHammerheadAttributes.x0` was renamed to `initial_velocity`, matching its use in the Hammer Head spawn velocity formula.
`itFoods_Logic18_Spawned` changed from `Item_GObj*` to `HSD_GObj*`, factored the random max into `getRandMax`, changed `PAD_STACK(12)` to `PAD_STACK(8)`, and removed casts at call sites.
`configure.py` marks `ithammerhead.c` as `Object(Matching, ...)`; `itsscopebeam.c` remains `NonMatching` in this diff despite new function matches.
Bot report: matched code +1192 bytes, linked code +1228 bytes, matched data +104 bytes, linked data +88 bytes for GALE01.
smart_moves: Introduced item-specific `scopebeam` item vars instead of continuing to borrow `starrodstar` storage, making future Super Scope beam work less error-prone.
Replaced raw special-attribute pointer arithmetic with `BeamFloats`/`ScopeBeamAttrs`, making velocity, scale, and lifetime semantics visible while still matching `it_80298ED0` and the anim callback.

Renamed the thrown callback from a generic bank-style name to the item-specific `itHammerHead_Logic40_Thrown`, then propagated it through symbols and logic tables so future searches find the owning item.
Used named `initial_velocity` in `itHammerheadAttributes`, documenting why the first attribute field participates in x/y velocity setup.
Kept compiler-sensitive forms visible in the C: explicit switch cases for scope beam item states, chained vector component assignments, and `PAD_STACK` adjustment in the food helper.
Updated symbol scope for matched `.sdata2` constants from global `it_804DCC60`-style names to local `@151`, `@152`, `@177`, and `@179`, consistent with newly matched local data.
decomp_lessons: When an item object becomes fully matching, update `configure.py` immediately from `NonMatching` to `Matching` so link/match metrics reflect the decomp work.
Typed article `x4_specialAttributes` structs can unlock both readability and matching by replacing casts and byte-offset indexing with field access.
For item callbacks, state transitions often require preserving the exact order of `ground_or_air` checks, `it_802762BC`, `it_8026B390`, and `Item_80268E5C` flags.
Projectile setup functions commonly pair `Item_80268B18` creation with owner/debug linkage via `db_80225DD8`; reuse that pattern only when the original evidence supports it.

Life-timer anim callbacks can match as small repeated patterns: decrement `xD44_lifeTimer`, return true on expiration, and call `it_802728C8` when below the shared warning threshold `it_804D6D28->x34`.
Do not leave generic item-var aliases in place once the owning item is identified; add a dedicated union member in `types.h` and a matching typedef in `itCommonItems.h`.
Symbol-map cleanup matters for data matching: local constants should be represented as local `@` labels when the matched object no longer needs global placeholder symbols.
naming_conventions: Hammer Head logic callbacks use `itHammerHead_Logic40_*`, e.g. `Spawned`, `PickedUp`, `Dropped`, `Thrown`, `DmgDealt`, and `DmgReceived`.
Hammer Head motion callbacks use `itHammerhead_UnkMotionN_Anim/Phys/Coll`; note the diff preserves the existing mixed capitalization `HammerHead` vs `Hammerhead`.
Unidentified functions remain address-based when semantics are still unclear, e.g. `it_80299C48` and `it_80299D7C`.
Attribute fields should be renamed when behavior is known: `itHammerheadAttributes.x0` became `initial_velocity`.
Item-var typedef/member naming follows item ownership: `itScopeBeam_ItemVars` and union member `scopebeam`.
Matched local constants in `symbols.txt` may use local labels like `@151` instead of global `it_804DCC60` placeholders.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: For Hammer Head spawn matching, initialize `SpawnItem` fields explicitly, zero all velocity components, copy `pos` into `prev_pos`, force `prev_pos.z = 0.0f`, and set both parent GObj fields before `Item_80268B18`.
Hammer Head velocity setup multiplies input velocity by `attrs->initial_velocity`, applies `it->facing_dir` to x velocity, and adds `0.5f` to y velocity.
`it_80299D7C` and `itHammerHead_Logic40_Thrown` use nearly identical ground-to-air setup, differing mainly in the `Item_80268E5C` state index and flags.
`it_80298ED0` keeps an explicit switch over scope beam kind values and calls `Item_80268E5C(projectile, N, ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE)` for each case rather than using a collapsed computed form.
Super Scope beam scale matching uses a local `Vec3 scale` with `scale.x = scale.y = scale.z = data->scale` followed by `HSD_JObjSetScale`.

The food routine match was helped by factoring `HSD_Randi(getRandMax(ip))` and adjusting `PAD_STACK(8)`, showing that tiny helper extraction and stack padding can be necessary for compiler equivalence.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in this slice, and the PR body was empty/null.

decomp.dev bot reported GALE01 improvements: matched code 55.27% (+0.03%, +1192 bytes), linked code 31.78% (+0.03%, +1228 bytes), matched data 34.59% (+0.01%, +104 bytes), linked data 29.17% (+0.01%, +88 bytes).

Bot listed 9 new matches: `itsscopebeam` `it_80298ED0`, Hammer Head `it_80299C48`, `itHammerhead_UnkMotion2_Anim`, `itHammerhead_UnkMotion3_Anim`, `it_80299D7C`, `itHammerHead_Logic40_Thrown`, Hammer Head `.data`, Hammer Head `.sdata2`, and Super Scope beam `.sdata`.
follow_up_queries: Search later PRs for completion of `src/melee/it/items/itsscopebeam.c` because this PR matched `it_80298ED0` but left the object marked `NonMatching`.
Check later naming cleanup for `it_80299C48`, `it_80299D7C`, `BeamFloats`, `ScopeBeamAttrs`, and `itScopeBeam_ItemVars.x0/x4`.
Look for additional PRs resolving Hammer Head `UnkMotion` names or further normalizing the `HammerHead`/`Hammerhead` capitalization split.
Verify whether later scope beam attribute typing changes the apparent `ScopeBeamAttrs.floats[9]` plus switch cases 0 through 9 pattern.
